commit.message:
feat: auto-create draft document on first autosave (#81)

## Summary

When an authenticated user starts typing without an active document, the
first autosave now creates a new draft in Sanity automatically. This
removes the friction of manually creating a document before writing.

## Changes

- **`HomeView.vue`**: Updated autosave logic to auto-create a draft via
`apiCreateDraft` when no `activeDocument` exists
- Added `latestJson` pattern to prevent stale content saves during async
operations

## Robustness

- **Serialized saves**: Uses `latestJson` + re-trigger in `finally`
blocks to ensure edits during draft creation or save are never dropped
- **Intro title detection**: Treats the intro placeholder title as
unset, falling back to 'Untitled'
- **Slug validation**: Falls back to `'untitled'` when `slugify()`
produces an empty string (e.g. emoji-only titles)
- **Empty title preservation**: Passes `undefined` (not empty string) as
title to save, preserving the draft's created title

## Testing

- All 119 tests pass (62 app + 57 API)
- Type-check clean, lint clean, pre-commit hooks pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move @azure/functions to dependencies and add publish feedback (#80)

## Summary

Fixes three production errors traced via Application Insights:

### Root cause (issues 1 & 2): `@azure/functions` was a devDependency

App Insights trace:
```
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package '@azure/functions'
imported from /home/site/wwwroot/dist/functions/createDraft.js
```

`@azure/functions` was listed as a `devDependency` in
`app/api/package.json`. The SWA deploy action prunes devDependencies, so
no function handlers could register at runtime — all `/api/*` endpoints
returned 404.

**Fix:** Moved `@azure/functions` from `devDependencies` to
`dependencies`.

### Issue 3: Publish button had no feedback

The `publishDocument()` function logged errors to the console but showed
nothing to the user.

**Fix:**
- Added `publishStatus` state to the editor store (mirrors the existing
`saveStatus` pattern)
- Unified status display in the menu bar — publish status takes priority
over save status
- Publish button is now properly disabled:
  - While a save is in progress (prevents race condition)
  - While a publish is in progress (prevents double-trigger)
- When the current document is not a draft (was previously a silent
no-op)

### Testing
- All 119 tests pass (66 API + 53 frontend)
- Type check passes
- ESLint passes
- Pre-commit hooks pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Volume normalization:
- Add DynamicsCompressor as soft limiter on master bus (blepInput → limiter → masterChannel)

* fix: replace inline static thread_local with function-local static in Model<>

Clang has a known bug where `inline static thread_local` members of class
templates produce a separate TLS variable per translation unit. This caused
`Model::the()` to throw "called outside of a simulation scope" on Clang even
when a `ModelScope` was active, because the scope wrote to one TU's copy of
`current_instance_` while `agent-meat.hpp` read from another TU's copy
(still nullptr).

The fix replaces the data member with a private static function holding a
function-local `thread_local` static. The C++ standard guarantees a single
instance per thread for function-local statics regardless of how many TUs
instantiate the template:

    static Model<TSeq> *& current_instance_() {
        thread_local Model<TSeq> * ptr = nullptr;
        return ptr;
    }

All three access sites are updated accordingly: Model::the(), Model::the_ptr(),
and ModelScope's constructor/destructor.

Also updates docs_src to clarify OpenMP usage (parallel runs only, not agent
loops) and adds a note on the infected-only sampling distinction in the
contact sampling documentation.

* Large system refactoring (addresses memory issues with ASan and simplifies the API). (#189)

## Major changes

### Refactoring

- Change the functionality of `Virus` and `Tool` functions: no longer capturing parameters via pointers (unsafe). Instead, switched to capturing parameter names via `std::shared_ptr` (validity checked in run-time).
- Replaced `GET_MODEL()` (`dynamic_cast`) macros with proper implementation of using `static_cast` for more efficient and easier to maintain models.
- The events are now part of `Model`. The event callback functions were passed via `std::function` to the `Event` class, and later dispatched by `Model`. There was no reason why to keep this design, so, instead, the update functions are called by enum (so instead of copying the function to the `Event` object, only an enum is passed), and then triggered as a private member of `Model`.
- The mixer functions used by the `Model` class are now member functions instead of member objects. Previously, these were stored as `MixerFun` objects, adding overhead and unnecessary dereferencing.
- The `Model::{array_double_tmp, array_virus_tmp}` are now proper arrays (so allocated in the stack) of 1024 x 2.

### Deleted/defunct code:

- Changed the functionality of the `Model::operator()` to not return a reference. This was unsafe behavior. Forcing users to call `Model::set_param()` instead.
- Removed unused (and redundant) constructor method for models. Left a single main constructor in place.
- The method `Agent::rm_agent_by_virus` is now `delete`d. It was not being used, and the method `Agent::rm_virus` already has the same functionality.
- The overhead of `ToolFunctions` that were shared between agents as a smart pointer has been eliminated. The functions are now stored as `ToolFun` objects in `Tool`. The same applies to `VirusFunctions`.

### Bugs

- The `Tool` and `Entity` classes lacked a `clone_ptr()` method. This was causing threads to share entities, which is most likely what triggered the ASan issues.
- One cryptic fix (proposed by Gemini, Codex, and Claude) was to free the memory of `Agent::tools` and `Agent::entities`. This change works with the code base and makes things safer (probably), so we kept it.

* Creates new Intervention PEP for the Measles models (#191)



## New intervention: PEP for Measles

Using the `GlobalEvent` as a base class, this PR creates a new feature that can be used with models involving post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP). In this case, we use composition and administer the PEP as either the Measles-Mumps-Rubella (MMR) vaccine or Immune Globulin (IG), depending on the agent's willingness to take it and timing. The model rationale is as follows:

1. The quarantine process runs. Agents who may be willing to use PEP are moved to quarantine for the moment.
2. The PEP intervention kicks in: Agents who (1) are willing to receive PEP and (2) are in either Quarantined Susceptible or Quarantined Exposed states are administered the PEP. These agents are also moved out of the quarantine process.
3. If exposition took place outside of the MMR window (usually 3 days), then IG is administered instead.
4. If the PEP is effective, exposed agents are moved to the recovered state.

The new feature can be applied as a simple `GlobalEvent` without having to modify the model.

In the case that all agents are willing to quarantine and take PEP, the transition matrix of the model should not feature any entry in the rows/columns of the Quarantine Exposed and Quarantined Susceptible states (this is included in the new test).

This PR includes two new tests: 14g and 14f for evaluating the Measles `InterventionMeaslesPEP` intervention on the `ModelMeaslesSchool`. The intervention can be added as follows:

```cpp
// Creating the PEP intervention and 
// setting it up so we can call it as a global event.
epimodels::InterventionMeaslesPEP<> pep(
  "Post-exposure prophylaxis for measles", // Name of the intervention
  1.0,       // "PEP MMR efficacy"
  1.0,       // "PEP IG efficacy"
  4.0 * 7.0, // "PEP IG half-life (mean)"
  7.0/2.0,   // "PEP IG half-life (sd)"
  1.0,       // "PEP willingness"
  3.0,       // "PEP MMR window"
  {MS::QUARANTINED_EXPOSED, MS::QUARANTINED_SUSCEPTIBLE},
  {MS::RECOVERED, MS::SUSCEPTIBLE}
);

pep.set_name("PEP intervention");

model.add_globalevent(pep);
```

Add evaluate-prompt-portability template, protocol, and format (#239)

* Add evaluate-prompt-portability template, protocol, and format

Adds three new PromptKit components for cross-LLM prompt portability
evaluation (addresses #127 Phase 1):

- Protocol: prompt-portability-evaluation — 7-phase claim-level
  consensus analysis methodology (output collection, claim extraction,
  semantic matching, consensus classification, divergence analysis,
  scoring, and hardening recommendations)

- Format: portability-report — 9-section structured report covering
  evaluation context, per-model summaries, consensus core, majority
  claims, divergent claims (singular + contradictory), scorecard,
  hardening recommendations, and model notes

- Template: evaluate-prompt-portability — interactive template that
  orchestrates fan-out execution across multiple LLM models, collects
  outputs, decomposes them into atomic semantic claims, performs
  cross-model consensus analysis, and produces a portability report

Key design: comparison is semantic (claim-level), not textual. Two
models producing the same assertions in different words score as
Consensus. Contradictory claims (mutually exclusive assertions) are
the highest-priority signal, traced to specific ambiguous prompt
language with concrete rewrite recommendations.

Complements the existing lint-prompt template — lint statically first,
then evaluate empirically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: scoring, thresholds, error handling

- Fix Majority threshold from >=50% to >50% to avoid ties with even
  model counts
- Normalize portability score from [-1,1] to [0,1] via
  (raw_weighted_mean + 1.0) / 2.0 so contradictory claims cannot
  produce negative scores
- Define explicit Manual Review bucket for uncertain claim matches:
  excluded from scoring, reported in new Uncertain / Needs Review
  section in the portability-report format
- Add fail-stop behavior when <2 models succeed: produce abbreviated
  report documenting failures instead of misleading partial analysis
- Add UR- claim ID prefix to formatting rules

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add reference model sufficiency analysis for model selection

Extends the portability evaluation with a new mode: when a reference
model is designated, its claims become the ground-truth baseline and
each cheaper model is scored on how well it reproduces that baseline.

Protocol (Phase 8 - Model Sufficiency Analysis):
- Per-model sufficiency rate = reproduced / total baseline claims
- Missing claims classified as critical miss vs minor miss
- Extra claims classified as valid addition / hallucination / noise
- Three-tier sufficiency status: sufficient, conditionally sufficient,
  insufficient (based on threshold, critical misses, contradictions)
- Identifies the minimum sufficient model (cheapest that meets
  threshold with zero critical misses)

Format (Section 10 - Model Sufficiency Matrix):
- Reference model + threshold display
- Per-model sufficiency table with tier, rates, and status
- Missing and extra claim detail tables
- Cost-efficiency recommendation

Template:
- New params: reference_model (optional), sufficiency_threshold
  (default 90%)
- Input validation ensures reference model is in the model list
- Step 7 conditionally applies Phase 8 when reference model is set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix template Steps 4-5 for Manual Review bucket consistency

Steps 4 and 5 were inconsistent with the protocol's Manual Review
bucket rules for uncertain matches. Step 4 only flagged uncertain
matches instead of placing them in a Manual Review bucket. Step 5
classified all clusters without excluding Manual Review clusters
from scoring.

Fixed:
- Step 4: uncertain matches now placed in Manual Review bucket,
  excluded from scored classification, reported under Uncertain /
  Needs Review section
- Step 5: classifies only non-Manual-Review clusters, includes
  Manual Review count in the summary presented to user

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix heading nesting, section omission rule, and cluster type ambiguity

- Fix 6c heading from ## to ### to match 6a/6b nesting under
  section 6 (Divergent Claims)
- Section 10 (Model Sufficiency Matrix) now always included per
  the format's 'do not omit any section' rule, with a placeholder
  when no reference model is designated
- Add canonical cluster type rule for scoring: when models assign
  different types to semantically matched claims, use the
  highest-weight type, break ties by majority, then arbiter decides.
  Original per-model types preserved for transparency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: auto-clean stale entities on integration reload

When sensors are removed in a newer version of cantera-ha, the old
entity registry entries now get cleaned up automatically on reload.
Previously the user had to delete and re-add the integration.

How it works:
- async_setup_entry initialises a 'current_unique_ids' set in hass.data
- Each platform (sensor, update, firmware_update) populates that set
  with the unique_ids of every entity it registers in this setup cycle
- After async_forward_entry_setups, _async_remove_stale_entities compares
  the entity registry against the set and removes any entries not in it
- User-disabled entities are never removed
- hass.data entry data is cleaned up on unload

Tests: 6 new tests covering stale removal, current entity preservation,
disabled entity protection, edge-case empty entry data, hass.data
initialisation, and unload cleanup. 180/180 passing.

commit.message:
[a11y] Fix `@elastic/eui/icon-accessibility-rules` violations across 55 kibana-presentation files (#260694)

Closes: https://github.com/elastic/kibana/issues/260637

Resolves 88 ESLint violations of `@elastic/eui/icon-accessibility-rules`
across 55 files owned by `@elastic/kibana-presentation`. Every `EuiIcon`
must have either an accessible label (`aria-label`, `title`, or
`aria-labelledby`) or be marked `aria-hidden={true}` if decorative.

## Strategy

**`aria-hidden={true}`** — applied to the majority of icons that are
decorative: drag handles inside labeled containers, icons in context
menu items (where the `name` field provides the label), icons alongside
visible text, and status icons inside tooltipped wrappers.

```tsx
// before
<EuiIcon type="grabHorizontal" />

// after — parent div already carries aria-label
<EuiIcon type="grabHorizontal" aria-hidden={true} />
```

**`aria-label` via `i18n.translate()`** — applied to icons that
independently convey meaning or are interactive:

| File | Icon | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `presentation_panel_title.tsx` | `info` with `tabIndex={0}` |
`aria-label="Description"` |
| `error_component.tsx` | `warning` with `onClick` | `aria-label="View
error details"` |
| `asset_picker.tsx` | `checkInCircleFilled` (selected state) |
`aria-label="Selected"` |
| `saved_query_management_list.tsx` | `info` / `clock` metadata
indicators | `aria-label="Has description"` / `aria-label="Has time
filter"` |

**`aria-label` on parent + `aria-hidden` on icon** —
`page_manager.component.tsx` had an unlabeled native `<button>` whose
only content was an icon; added `aria-label` to the button and
`aria-hidden={true}` to the icon.

---------

commit.message:
fix(e2e): add CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL to debt-service in docker-compose

The debt-service was missing CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL in its Docker
environment, causing it to use the default http://localhost:8092 —
which inside the Docker network points to the container itself, not
the creditor-service container.

This caused validateCreditorAction() to fail with a connection error
on every claim submission, triggering the Resilience4j circuit breaker
fallback that threw OpenDebtException(CRITICAL), which GlobalExceptionHandler
mapped to HTTP 503. The creditor-portal then showed AFVIST with
'503: Debt service unavailable', failing FR-P012-02b.

Fix: add CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL: http://creditor-service:8092 alongside
the already-correct CASE_SERVICE_URL pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: model fallback chain — auto-downgrade on rate limit errors

New src/models.ts defines the priority chain:
  claude-opus-4.6-1m (6 RPM) → claude-opus-4.6 (3 RPM) → gpt-5.4 (1 RPM)
